common.tagline: for lazy people who don't give upis.
common.tagline_note: ("upis" is not a typo — it's a playful suffix inspired by Mussum, a beloved Brazilian comedian. Think of it as our cultural Easter egg.)

session.no_session_description: Start a session to track your focus and get back on track when you drift.
session.no_sessions_yet: No sessions yet. Start your first focus session!
session.sync_error: LazyNevis couldn't confirm the current session state.

charts.no_data: No data yet for this period

history.title: Session History
history.empty: No sessions found. Start your first session!
history.delete_confirm: Delete this session? This cannot be undone.
history.clear_confirm: Delete ALL sessions? This cannot be undone.

export.sensitive_warning: Exports may contain window titles, application activity, and checkpoint text. Review them before sharing.

settings.title: Settings
settings.save_failed: Settings couldn't be saved. Your current screen values are still available.
settings.load_failed: Settings couldn't be loaded from local storage.

section_desc.general: Language, theme, and window monitoring parameters.
section_desc.focus_rules: Define which apps count as 'focus' and which are 'distractions'. Rules apply immediately — even during an active session.
section_desc.alerts: Configure when and how LazyNevis reminds you to refocus. Alerts only fire during a session, after you've been in a distraction app longer than the configured threshold.
section_desc.audio: Audio cues play when alerts fire. Use the built-in sounds or pick any MP3, WAV, or OGG file from your computer.
section_desc.breaks: Break reminders nudge you to step away after long focus streaks. Compatible with Pomodoro (25 min focus + 5 min break).
section_desc.shortcuts: Global keyboard shortcuts work from any app — no need to open LazyNevis first.
section_desc.data: All data is stored locally on your device. Nothing is ever sent to external servers.

general.polling_interval_hint: How often LazyNevis checks which app is active. Lower = more responsive, higher = less CPU. Min: 500 ms.
general.micro_threshold_hint: Window switches shorter than this are ignored. Prevents noise from accidental clicks or quick alt-tabs.
general.start_minimized_hint: When enabled, LazyNevis starts silently in the system tray without showing the window.
general.launch_at_login_hint: Automatically starts LazyNevis when you sign in to Windows, macOS, or Linux. Login launches always start in the tray.

focus_rules.mode_allowlist: Allowlist — only apps in the list count as focus
focus_rules.mode_blocklist: Blocklist — only apps in the list count as distractions
focus_rules.mode_allowlist_desc: Allowlist: only apps you list are focus — everything else is a distraction.
focus_rules.mode_blocklist_desc: Blocklist: only apps you list are distractions — everything else is focus.
focus_rules.apps_hint: Type an app name or pick from the running apps below. Only the executable name is stored (e.g. code, chrome.exe).
focus_rules.browser_rules_hint: When a browser is open, LazyNevis checks the tab title to decide if it's focus or distraction. Only applies to browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Edge, etc.).
focus_rules.tab_mode_allowlist_desc: Allowlist — only keywords below are focus

alerts.notification_desc: A native OS notification. Subtle — won't interrupt your workflow.
alerts.fullscreen_desc: A full-screen flashing overlay. Aggressive — hard to ignore. Requires a click to dismiss.
alerts.cooldown_hint: After dismissing an alert, LazyNevis won't fire another one for this duration. Prevents spam.
alerts.threshold_hint: Time in distraction before alert fires (seconds)

audio.notification_sound_desc: Plays once when the desktop notification fires.
audio.fullscreen_sound_desc: Loops continuously while the fullscreen overlay is visible. Stops when you dismiss it.
audio.test_hint: Alert logic: fires when distracted_for ≥ threshold AND cooldown has passed. Checked every second.

breaks.focus_interval_hint: How long you need to be in focus before a break reminder fires.
breaks.break_duration_hint: Shown in the break countdown on the dashboard.
breaks.hint: Compatible with Pomodoro: 25 min focus + 5 min break.

shortcuts.toggle_focus_hint: Starts a new session or pauses/resumes the current one.
shortcuts.stop_session_hint: Ends and saves the active focus session.
shortcuts.add_checkpoint_hint: Adds a checkpoint to the active session.
shortcuts.instructions_before: Select a field and press a key combination, or
shortcuts.instructions_after: to cancel. Shortcuts require Ctrl/Cmd, Alt, or Shift.
shortcuts.save_failed: That shortcut couldn't be registered. The previous shortcuts remain active.
shortcuts.startup_failed: Saved shortcuts couldn't be registered on this computer. Review them in Settings.

data.clear_history_confirm: This will permanently delete all your session history. Are you sure?
data.destructive_warning: Destructive actions below cannot be undone.

permissions.title: App Permissions
permissions.intro: LazyNevis needs the following permissions to work correctly. Grant them here, or revisit this screen at any time in Settings.
permissions.all_ok: All required permissions are granted. LazyNevis is fully operational.

notifications.label: Notifications
notifications.desc: Required to show focus alerts as native desktop notifications when you drift away from your work.

accessibility.label: Automation (macOS)
accessibility.desc: Required to detect which app is active and read window titles. Without this, window monitoring won't work on macOS. Grant in System Settings → Privacy & Security → Automation.

permissions.section_desc: Manage the OS permissions LazyNevis needs. You can re-request any permission from here without restarting the app.

alerts.notification_body: You've been on {{app}} for {{time}}. Get back on track!
alerts.break_notification_body: You've been focused for {{time}}. Take a short break.

app.close_warning_body: You have an active focus session. Closing the app will end it and save your progress.
app.close_note: Session ended when the app window closed

about.title: About LazyNevis
about.description: LazyNevis is a lightweight, open-source desktop focus tool. It lives in your tray, watches what you're doing, and nudges you back on track — without blocking or judging.
about.tagline_body: LazyNevis is a playful take on the word "lazy" — a nod to all of us who procrastinate but don't give up. The "-is" suffix is inspired by Mussum, a beloved Brazilian comedian known for his quirky speech patterns. It's a cultural Easter egg, not a typo.
about.built_with: Built with Tauri, Rust, React, and ♥
about.update_failed: Couldn't check for updates. Check your connection and try again.
